channel.title: SevenAtSevenSection1
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenSection1, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: How to register to vote in the 2026 primaries
item.description: For the 2026 primary elections, NPR has collected deadlines and information on how to register to vote — online, in person or by mail — in every U.S. ...

item.title: Israel will begin contentious West Bank land registration
item.description: Israel will begin a contentious land regulation process in a large part of the occupied West Bank, which could result in Israel gaining control over w...

item.title: Is that carb ultra-processed? Here's a test even a kid can do
item.description: The latest nutrition guidelines urge Americans to avoid highly-processed food. But, when it comes to carbs, many people don't know which ones are ultr...

item.title: 'American Struggle' author assesses Trump's expansion of presidential power
item.description: Pulitzer Prize-winning historian Jon Meacham talks about Trump's impact on democracy. Meacham's latest book is a collection of speeches, letters and o...

item.title: U.S. Olympic speed skaters adapt NASCAR 'bump drafting,' revolutionizing team event
item.description: U.S. Team Pursuit speed skaters will top speeds of 30 mph by pushing themselves around the track mere inches from each other.

item.title: African defensetech Terra Industries, founded by two Gen Zers, raises additional $22M in a month
item.description: Terra Industries, the African defense company, announced Monday that it had secured an additional $22 million in funding to further expand the busines...

item.title: Blackstone backs Neysa in up to $1.2B financing as India pushes to build domestic AI infrastructure
item.description: Neysa is targeting deployments of more than 20,000 GPUs over time as demand for local AI compute accelerates.

item.title: As AI data centers hit power limits, Peak XV backs Indian startup C2i to fix the bottleneck
item.description: C2i has raised $15 million as it tests a grid-to-GPU approach to reducing power losses in AI data centers.

item.title: No clear path to ending the partial government shutdown as lawmakers dig in over DHS oversight
DNA recovered from glove found near Guthrie home that appears to match glove worn by suspect, FBI says
item.description: The FBI says a glove containing DNA was found about two miles from Nancy Guthrie’s Arizona home and appears to match those worn by a masked person out...

item.title: OpenClaw creator Peter Steinberger joins OpenAI
item.description: OpenAI said OpenClaw will live on as an open source project.

item.title: Longtime NPR host David Greene sues Google over NotebookLM voice
item.description: The longtime host of NPR’s “Morning Edition,” is suing Google, alleging that the male podcast voice in the company’s NotebookLM tool is based on him.

item.title: Trump’s border czar says ‘small’ security force will remain in Minnesota after enforcement drawdown
How to (Legally) Pay the Least Amount in Taxes as a Real Estate Investor
item.description: This episode alone could save you hundreds, thousands, or tens of thousands in taxes—all with 100% legal means. If you own a rental property, you coul...

item.title: How to Buy Rental #2, #3, or #4 When You’re Out of Funds (Rookie Reply)
item.description: Stuck at one rental property? Maybe you spent years saving for that first down payment, and now, your funds are depleted. Where do you go from here? N...

item.title: Grand Rapids Could Become a Boomtown as Investment Money Pours In
item.description: Looking for an affordable, cash-flowing market earmarked for major development and a projected population and economic surge? Grand Rapids, Michigan, ...

item.title: Buying (and Building) Houses Could Get a LOT Easier (New Bill)
item.description: This could be the most important shift in the housing market in years. Something truly remarkable just happened that will impact almost every facet of...

item.title: Over 60% of Buyers Bought Below Asking Price Last Year, With the Largest Discounts Since 2012
item.description: For most of us, the frenzied bidding wars and constant price hikes of the post-pandemic housing boom are recent memories. That’s why it might come as ...
